concepts.title: Types of Human Settlements
concepts.description: There are three main types of human settlements: rural, urban, and tribal. Rural settlements are found in rural areas and are often agricultural-based, while urban settlements are found in cities and are typically industrial-based. Tribal settlements are found in remote areas and are often based on traditional practices.

concepts.title: Characteristics of Rural Settlements
concepts.description: Rural settlements are typically found in rural areas, are often agricultural-based, and have a low population density. They usually have a village layout and are based on local culture and traditions.

concepts.title: Factors Influencing Human Settlements
concepts.description: Human settlements are influenced by several factors, including climate, soil, water, and natural resources. These factors determine the location, size, and type of settlement.

concepts.title: Types of Urban Settlements
concepts.description: The types of urban settlements can be remembered using the acronym CAMP: Conurbation (a large urban area with a high population density), Agglomeration (a collection of urban areas around a central city), Metropolis (a large city with a high population density), and Peripheral (a settlement located on the outskirts of a city).

concepts.title: Confusion between Rural and Urban Settlements
concepts.description: Students often confuse rural and urban settlements. To avoid this mistake, remember that rural settlements are found in rural areas and are often agricultural-based, while urban settlements are found in cities and are typically industrial-based.

concepts.title: Growth of Urban Settlements
concepts.description: The growth of urban settlements can be measured in terms of population growth, economic growth, and infrastructure development. Students should remember that the growth of urban settlements is often driven by factors such as industrialization, urbanization, and migration.

concepts.title: Characteristics of Tribal Settlements
concepts.description: Tribal settlements are typically found in remote areas, are often based on traditional practices, and have a low population density. They usually have a small population and are often self-sufficient.

concepts.title: Factors Influencing the Location of Human Settlements
concepts.description: The location of human settlements is influenced by several factors, including climate, soil, water, and natural resources. These factors determine the suitability of an area for human habitation.
